title: FAA Commercial Space Transportation License
text: Vehicle operator license covers launch + reentry. Application must include Flight Safety Analysis (FSA) demonstrating expected casualty Ec ≤ 1e-4 collective (members of the public) per mission. License covers operational area + flight corridor. Renewable every 5 years; performance reporting required.

title: Flight Safety Analysis methodology
text: Required components: trajectory analysis, debris analysis (post-explosion + intact reentry), populated area analysis, expected casualty calc, debris dispersion footprint, gas + blast hazard, toxic release. Public risk policy thresholds: Ec ≤ 1e-4 collective, ≤ 1e-6 individual per launch.

title: Flight termination system
text: FTS required for any vehicle whose nominal trajectory crosses populated areas. Two-string voting + redundancy required for AFTS. Qualification per RCC 319-19: environmental (vibration, thermal, EMC), functional, and end-to-end test. Hot-fire test before integration. AFTS unit + ground equipment certified together.

title: NASA Range Flight Safety Program
text: Range safety for NASA-owned + tenant operations. Risk methodology incorporates FSA, hazard areas, public risk. Mission Flight Control Officer (MFCO) authorized to terminate flight. Risk caps mirror FAA. Coordination with USCG NOTMAR + FAA NOTAM for public notification.

title: ITAR controls on spacecraft technical data
text: Spacecraft + associated equipment + technical data + defense services controlled. Export (including disclosure to foreign persons) requires authorization: DSP-5 license, TAA (Technical Assistance Agreement), or specific exemption. Foreign-person disclosure of USML XV technical data without TAA = ITAR violation; civil penalty up to $1.2M/violation.

title: EAR controls on dual-use spacecraft items
text: ECCN 9A515 covers spacecraft + payload components moved from USML to EAR by 2014 export control reform. License determinant: end-use, end-user, destination. CCATS (Commodity Classification Automated Tracking System) for classification. Reexport authorization required when transferring to second country.

title: 25-year LEO disposal rule
text: Post-mission disposal for LEO (≤2000 km altitude): controlled deorbit OR orbital lifetime ≤25 years (re-entry within 25 years of mission end). FCC has signaled tightening to 5 years for FCC-licensed satellites (2022 R&O). IADC + ISO 24113. Operator must demonstrate disposal capability in license application.

title: Conjunction screening
text: Satellite operators receive Conjunction Data Messages (CDMs) from 18 SDS via Space-Track.org. Operators must evaluate maneuver decision when Pc (probability of collision) exceeds threshold (commonly 1e-4). Collision-on-launch screening — verify launch trajectory does not conjunct active objects within risk window.

title: Financial responsibility / MPL
text: Operator must obtain maximum probable loss (MPL) insurance covering third-party + government property. FAA AST determines MPL via methodology in regulation. Indemnification cap $500M (statutory). MPL bond / certificate must be on file before license issuance. Periodic reassessment.

title: Two-person concurrent verification on propellant
text: Propellant loading + ordnance work requires two qualified personnel with independent task verification (concurrent verification). Single-person propellant operations = critical safety violation across DoD ranges + FAA-licensed sites. Logged + signed by both at each critical step.

title: Hazard area + public clearance
text: Operator must define ship + aircraft + land hazard areas based on FSA. Public clearance verified through NOTMAR (Coast Guard), NOTAM (FAA), and ground exclusion. Verbal-only clearance is non-compliant. Hazard area boundaries depend on vehicle, propellant mass, and debris footprint.
